fields.description:
## Context

The plan-review gate's re-review of an *edited* plan does not converge: each remediation
round can surface new issues in previously-clean criteria, expanding scope every run and
creating a non-terminating loop that drives cost, wall-clock, and agent non-compliance.
This is distinct from run-over-run reproducibility on fixed input (the LLM-as-judge
self-consistency problem); the plan legitimately changed, so feedback *should* differ. The
problem we solve: a remediation re-review should confirm the surfaced issues were resolved
**without** introducing different, lower-stakes issues that restart the loop.

Prior conversation rejected three approaches: per-diff-region scoping (new section-diffing
machinery), skip-clean-criteria reuse (unsound — reuses absence-of-finding, freezes
high-stakes defects an edit introduces), and the whole-verdict probe (too coarse). This
epic captures the chosen approach. Relates to the plan-review epic `5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa`.
It is decomposed into 6 child tasks (see "Decomposition" below).

## Terminology (canonical)

- **impact** — the verifier's coarse severity-attribute score in [0,1] (`decide.impact`).
- **validity** — the graded fraction of the Pass-2 binary sub-answers in [0,1].
- **priority** — `validity × impact` (the existing Pass-3 score; the drop axis).
- **novelty score** — a new [0,1] score derived deterministically from a new Pass-2
  novelty sub-answer set (see below); high = "not present in the prior review".
- **carryover finding** — a current finding matching a prior-review finding (low novelty).

## What (the chosen design)

A **rising-floor remediation re-review**: run the FULL criteria set every time (no skipped
criteria, no Pass-1 anchoring → full recall), and on a remediation re-review apply a
deterministic Pass-3 floor that drops only **novel, low-priority** findings.

- **Full Pass-1, unanchored.** Every applicable criterion runs; the finder never sees prior
  findings (avoids the sycophancy/anchoring the research documents). High-priority defects
  an edit introduces are still found — nothing is frozen.
- **Novelty as a narrow-question score, in a SEPARATE Pass-2 sub-call.** Novelty is scored
  by a DISTINCT structured sub-call that ALONE receives the prior findings and answers a
  small novelty sub-answer set ("does this finding correspond to a prior finding?"). The
  existing verification sub-call (severity attributes + the binary/validity sub-answers)
  receives **NO** prior findings, so the `verify.py` independence invariant
  (VERIFIER_RULES[0]) is enforced *structurally*, not by prompt assertion — mirroring the
  Pass-1 isolation. Pass-3 maps the novelty sub-answers to a deterministic novelty score,
  exactly as it maps the binary sub-answers to validity.
- **Deterministic rising floor (Pass-3).** A finding is dropped iff novelty ≥ `T_novel` AND
  `priority < floor`. Carryover findings (low novelty) use the normal threshold and must
  still be resolved. A novel high-priority finding (high impact × high validity) is
  preserved (and may block). The whole policy is deterministic — no LLM holistic severity.
- **Freshness window = remediation-mode gate.** The floor applies only when: config enabled,
  plan changed, code unchanged since baseline, registry unchanged, a prior sidecar (with
  finding text) is available, and the last review (full OR partial) is within the window
  (default 60 min). **Each review resets the window**, so the loop persists across a series
  of edits while the agent is actively iterating; it lapses to a normal full (un-floored)
  review only after the agent goes idle past the window. Code-changed / both-drifted → full
  review (`drift_refresh` untouched — out of scope). **Back-out:** setting the config key off
  restores byte-identical full-review behavior.

## Narrowed-verdict / sidecar schema

- The verdict's `coverage` records `narrowed: true`, `floored_criteria`, and the floored
  finding ids when the rising floor was applied (absent/false on a normal review).
- Dropped novel findings are written to the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (not the surfaced
  verdict), joinable by `norm_id`, so suppression is always observable.

## Defaults & calibration (falsifiable)

- Starting defaults (tunable): `T_novel = 0.7`; `priority floor = the p40 impact percentile
  of the 245-event corpus × validity` (≈ the "below major" band). Both are *derived*, not
  guessed: `scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py` prints the percentile inputs.
- The novelty scorer must clear an **evidence gate** (a measured carryover-vs-novel
  discrimination bar on labeled pairs) BEFORE the Pass-3 drop rule is activated.

## Within-session suppression (deliberate, honest bound)

Because the window resets on each review, a novel low-priority finding can be re-floored
every round while the agent keeps iterating — within-session suppression is **intentionally
unbounded** (the chosen persistence behavior). It is bounded only by: the idle-lapse window,
carryover findings still being enforced at the normal bar, and every dropped finding being
recorded in the sidecar. Optional future safety valve: a `max_consecutive_narrowed_rounds`
cap that forces a full un-floored review. (No unsound *coverage* accumulates — Pass-1 always
runs the full criteria set; only the surfacing of novel low-priority findings is suppressed.)

## Empirical grounding (established)

- 245 `REVIEW_RESULT` events / 2,571 findings: the verifier's impact axis already
  discriminates (15% critical, 40% none, mean impact 0.43; attributes spread sensibly) — so
  impact discrimination is not a blocker; the floor has real spread to act on.
- The impact prompt nonetheless gives the severity attributes zero anchoring (vs rich
  guidance for validity) → tightening it is a robustness item, not a blocker.

## Already landed (observability foundation)

- `scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py` — repeatable corpus distribution report.
- `discriminates_impact_levels` eval scorer + `high_impact`/`low_impact` dataset pairs on
  `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`.

## Decomposition (6 child tasks)

1. Persist finding text in the sidecar + latest-sidecar reader (foundation).
2. Pass-2 novelty sub-answers in a SEPARATE sub-call + a `discriminates_novelty` eval gate.
3. Pass-3 novelty score + rising-floor drop rule (depends on 2; gated by the eval bar).
4. Remediation-mode gate: freshness window + preconditions + wiring (depends on 1).
5. Tighten the verifier impact prompt (robustness; verified by the impact eval).
6. Docs + ADR + offline convergence tests (depends on 3, 4).

## Scope

- IN: full-run + deterministic rising floor; separate-sub-call novelty scoring + its eval
  gate; freshness-window remediation-mode gating; sidecar finding-text persistence;
  impact-prompt tightening; config keys; docs + ADR.
- OUT: the code-drift `drift_refresh` path (unchanged); per-diff-region scoping;
  skip-clean-criteria reuse.

## Success Criteria

- A remediation re-review (pure plan edit, code unchanged, within window) runs the full
  criteria set, drops only novel low-priority findings, keeps carryover + novel high-priority
  findings, and reaches a signed PASS in bounded rounds (convergence to a PASS — *not* a
  bound on within-session suppression of low-priority findings).
- Severity/validity independence is enforced structurally (novelty in a separate Pass-2
  sub-call); Pass-1 receives no prior findings.
- Any precondition failing → byte-identical full review; `drift_refresh` behavior unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sidecar `_slim` persists `finding`, `suggested_fix`, `checklist_item` (sidecar event
      only; surfaced verdict shape byte-unchanged); a latest-sidecar reader exists; the
      `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` retention bound is respected.
- [ ] Pass-2 scores novelty in a SEPARATE structured sub-call that alone receives prior
      findings; the severity/validity sub-call receives none (independence enforced
      structurally). Pass-1 receives no prior findings.
- [ ] A `discriminates_novelty` eval scorer + labeled (prior-finding, new-finding)
      carryover/novel pairs land on `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`; the scorer clears its
      bar before the Pass-3 drop rule is activated (the evidence gate).
- [ ] Pass-3: deterministic novelty score; drop iff novelty ≥ `T_novel` AND priority < floor;
      carryover → normal threshold; novel high-priority → preserved; dropped → sidecar.
- [ ] Defaults committed: `T_novel = 0.7` and the priority floor at the stated corpus
      percentile (config-overridable); the distribution script prints the inputs.
- [ ] Remediation-mode gate: config key (default-on) + window minutes (default 60, measured
      from the last review of any kind and reset each review); code-unchanged precondition;
      both-drifted → full review; setting the key off restores byte-identical full review.
- [ ] Coverage on a narrowed verdict sets `narrowed: true` + `floored_criteria` + floored
      finding ids; dropped novel findings are written to the sidecar.
- [ ] Impact-prompt tightening is verified by the `discriminates_impact_levels` eval scoring
      at or above its threshold on the existing high/low pairs (not an effort claim).
- [ ] Offline FakeRunner tests: convergence on a remediation edit; each precondition fallback
      to a byte-identical full review; novel high-priority preserved; carryover enforced.
- [ ] Docs: a `docs/plan-review-gate.md` section AND an ADR under `docs/adr/` recording the
      chosen design, the three rejected alternatives, and the invariants (Pass-1 unanchored;
      novelty in a separate Pass-2 sub-call; drop decision deterministic in Pass-3).

## Out of scope / explicitly deferred

- Any change to the code-drift `drift_refresh` path.
- A `max_consecutive_narrowed_rounds` cap on within-session suppression (optional future
  safety valve; the chosen design accepts unbounded within-session suppression while the
  agent is actively iterating).
